content.thinking:
Now I understand the setup:
- App label: `app: demo-app`
- Container port: 5678
- The app serves at root "/" and returns "hello"

The task requires exposing at `http://demo.example.com/api`, but the app only serves "/". I need to use nginx's rewrite functionality to strip the `/api` prefix before forwarding to the app.

Let me create:
1. A Service that targets port 5678 on pods with `app: demo-app`
2. An Ingress with nginx class that routes `demo.example.com/api` to the service, with a rewrite to "/"
3. The nginx rewrite annotation will handle stripping the `/api` prefix so requests reach the app's root path

, which serves the index.html file. Now I'll write out the ingress and deployment manifests.

content.text: The app uses label `app: demo-app` on port 5678 and only serves `/`. I need to rewrite `/api` → `/` in the ingress. Creating Service and Ingress now.

input.command:
kubectl apply -f - <<'EOF'
apiVersion: v1
kind: Service
metadata:
  name: demo-app
  namespace: demo
spec:
  selector:
    app: demo-app
  ports:
    - name: http
      port: 80
      targetPort: 5678
---
apiVersion: networking.k8s.io/v1
kind: Ingress
metadata:
  name: demo-app
  namespace: demo
  annotations:
    nginx.ingress.kubernetes.io/rewrite-target: /
spec:
  ingressClassName: nginx
  rules:
    - host: demo.example.com
      http:
        paths:
          - path: /api
            pathType: Prefix
            backend:
              service:
                name: demo-app
                port:
                  number: 80
EOF
input.description: Create Service and Ingress for demo-app

input.command: kubectl -n demo get ingress demo-app && sleep 3 && kubectl -n demo exec curl-test -- curl -sS -H "Host: demo.example.com" http://ingress-nginx-controller.ingress-nginx.svc/api
input.description: Verify ingress and run the test request
